item.title: The force that isn’t a force
item.description: It is an emergent phenomenon where systems spontaneously order themselves, such as dice aligning when shaken or rubber bands springing back, not from a physical pull, but because higher entropy (more configurations) is statistically favored

media:content.media:title: The force that isn’t a force
media:content.media:description: It is an emergent phenomenon where systems spontaneously order themselves, such as dice aligning when shaken or rubber bands springing back, not from a physical pull, but because higher entropy (more configurations) is statistically favored

item.title: Impossible Domino
item.description: I show you a domino that stands back up after falling over and hitting the domino next to it.

media:content.media:title: Impossible Domino
media:content.media:description: I show you a domino that stands back up after falling over and hitting the domino next to it.

item.title: Boyle’s self-flowing flask experiment
item.description: In this video I combine Robert Boyle's self flowing flask with Polyethylene Gycol, the self pouring or self siphoning liquid in order to attempt a perpetual motion flow system. I make the internet version of Boyle's flask to attempt this then I show the neat properties of the polyethylene glycol. Then talk about how perpetual motion is actually possible with superfluids.

media:content.media:title: Boyle’s self-flowing flask experiment
media:content.media:description: In this video I combine Robert Boyle's self flowing flask with Polyethylene Gycol, the self pouring or self siphoning liquid in order to attempt a perpetual motion flow system. I make the internet version of Boyle's flask to attempt this then I show the neat properties of the polyethylene glycol. Then talk about how perpetual motion is actually possible with superfluids.

item.title: Carrying water in a sieve
item.description: In this video I show you how to carry water in a sieve using hydrophobicity

media:content.media:title: Carrying water in a sieve
media:content.media:description: In this video I show you how to carry water in a sieve using hydrophobicity

item.title: Trapping an electron in a box
item.description: The Action Lab explores a physics experiment about trapping an electron in a box and what it can reveal about light and science.

media:content.media:title: Trapping an electron in a box
media:content.media:description: The Action Lab explores a physics experiment about trapping an electron in a box and what it can reveal about light and science.

item.title: Swimming in Syrup vs. Water
item.description: In this video I test out the proposition that swimming in syrup is as easy as swimming in water. Based on a research paper published at the University of Minnesota

media:content.media:title: Swimming in Syrup vs. Water
media:content.media:description: In this video I test out the proposition that swimming in syrup is as easy as swimming in water. Based on a research paper published at the University of Minnesota
